content.title: Are you fasting the 6 days of Shawwal?
content.subtitle: Fasting 6 days in Shawwal after Ramadhan equals the reward of an entire year!
content.body:
The Virtue of 6 Salat in Shawwal

A significant voluntary act in the month of Shawwal is keeping 6 fasts. Fasting 6 days in Shawwal after the month of Ramadhan is equivalent to fasting the entire year! In our society, some refer to these as "Witness Fasts," believing they will testify before Allah regarding one's Ramadhan fasts. This belief is groundless and incorrect. No such concept of "Witness Fasts" exists in the Quran or authentic Hadith, so we should refrain from such beliefs.

Fasts of Ayyam al-Bidh

The 13th, 14th, and 15th of every lunar month are known as Ayyam al-Bidh (The White Days). Numerous authentic Hadith encourage fasting during these three days. The Prophet (SAW) fasted regularly on these days and encouraged his companions to do the same. Fasting three days every lunar month is equivalent to the reward of fasting a whole year.

Dates for Ayyam al-Bidh in Shawwal

For the current month of Shawwal, the Ayyam al-Bidh fasts should be observed on April 2, 3, and 4, 2026 (Thursday, Friday, and Saturday) in Bangladesh. For other countries, please check our calendar page to follow the Hijri date for 13, 14 and 15 Shawwal.

Can the 6 Fasts of Shawwal and Ayyam al-Bidh be Combined?

Both the 6 fasts of Shawwal and the 3 fasts of Ayyam al-Bidh carry immense rewards. For those who are able, keeping a total of 9 fasts separately is the most virtuous approach.

If 9 days aren't possible, you can achieve the rewards of 3 Sunnahs by keeping just 6 fasts through intentional planning:

  • The Strategy: Keep your 6 fasts on the 3 days of Ayyam al-Bidh (13th, 14th, and 15th of the lunar month) and the remaining 3 days on Mondays and Thursdays.

  • The Impact: By doing this, you can hope to attain the rewards for the 6 fasts of Shawwal, the Ayyam al-Bidh fasts, and the Monday/Thursday Sunnah fasts all at once, InshaAllah.

Don't miss this opportunity to maximize your rewards with smart effort. May Allah accept our post-Ramadhan deeds. Ameen.
